daily_brief: Escalating US-Iran hostilities dominated the day's headlines as Washington launched its eighth consecutive night of strikes targeting Iran's Revolutionary Guard following the deaths of two American service members in Jordan [France 24, BBC, Globe and Mail]. Iran retaliated with drone attacks on US bases and infrastructure in Kuwait, threatening a 'full-scale offensive' if strikes continue [RFI, The Hindu]. The conflict over the Strait of Hormuz has collapsed an interim deal signed just weeks ago. In Eastern Europe, Russia launched what Ukraine's foreign minister called the largest ballistic missile attack on Kyiv since the war began, killing one and wounding at least 13, one day after Ukrainian drones struck e-commerce warehouses near Moscow killing eight [Al Jazeera, France 24, BBC]. In a significant political development, Hungary's President Tamás Sulyok agreed to step down after parliament passed a constitutional amendment forcing his removal, marking the most dramatic move yet by Prime Minister Péter Magyar's new government [BBC]. Meanwhile, controversial influencer Andrew Tate and his brother Tristan were arrested in Miami as British prosecutors seek extradition on 59 charges including rape and sex trafficking [BBC, Globe and Mail, France 24]. On a lighter note, Spain and Argentina prepare to meet in the World Cup final in New York, with wildfire haze from Canadian fires hanging over the eastern US [Le Monde, France 24].

top_stories.headline: US Launches Eighth Night of Strikes on Iran After Two American Soldiers Killed in Jordan
bullets: US Central Command confirmed two American service members were killed and one remains missing after an Iranian drone and missile attack on a base in Jordan on Friday
The US launched strikes targeting Iran's Revolutionary Guard, coastal surveillance facilities, air defense systems, and missile storage sites overnight Saturday
Iran retaliated with drone attacks on two US military bases in Kuwait and struck power plants and oil facilities there, threatening a 'full-scale offensive' if US strikes continue
An interim deal signed June 14 aimed at ending hostilities over the Strait of Hormuz has effectively collapsed, with Iran suspending its commitments

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Emphasized the live, developing nature of events and Lebanese diplomatic efforts

sources.name: BBC
sources.framing_note: Detailed military terminology and infrastructure targets

sources.name: Globe and Mail
sources.framing_note: Focused on the collapse of diplomatic efforts and regional implications

sources.name: RFI
sources.framing_note: Highlighted Iranian threats and Gulf state responses

sources.name: The Hindu
sources.framing_note: Provided extensive context on the Strait of Hormuz dispute and historical background

sources.name: Al Jazeera
sources.framing_note: Brief coverage framing strikes as Trump's 'swift punishment'

top_stories.headline: Russia Launches Largest Ballistic Missile Attack on Kyiv Since War Began
bullets: Russian ballistic missiles struck six districts of Kyiv early Sunday, killing at least one person and wounding 13-15 others
The attack came one day after Ukrainian drones struck two Wildberries e-commerce warehouses near Moscow, killing eight people and injuring 62
Ukraine's Foreign Minister described it as the largest ballistic missile attack on the capital since the war started
Ukraine faces acute shortages of Patriot air defense missiles needed to counter ballistic missile threats

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Noted dual challenge of Russian attacks and domestic political instability over military leadership changes

sources.name: Al Jazeera
sources.framing_note: Emphasized Ukraine's vulnerability due to Patriot missile shortages and domestic tensions over defense minister removal

sources.name: BBC
sources.framing_note: Focused on the warehouse strikes and Wildberries as Russia's largest online retailer

sources.name: The Hindu
sources.framing_note: Straightforward factual coverage of casualties and damage

top_stories.headline: Andrew Tate and Brother Arrested in Miami as UK Seeks Extradition on 59 Charges
bullets: US Marshals arrested Andrew Tate, 39, and brother Tristan in Miami on Saturday on a sealed warrant
UK Crown Prosecution Service announced 59 total charges: 42 against Andrew including seven counts of rape, and 17 against Tristan including two counts of rape
Charges include rape, sex trafficking, assault, tax evasion, money laundering, and offenses related to indecent images of a child
The alleged offenses occurred between July 2010 and August 2017 in an area north of London; the brothers also face separate charges in Romania

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Described Tate as a 'self-professed misogynist' and 'radical misogynist influencer'

sources.name: BBC
sources.framing_note: Comprehensive legal detail, noted brothers' previous denials of wrongdoing

sources.name: Globe and Mail
sources.framing_note: Emphasized their social media empire promoting 'male dominance and misogyny'

sources.name: Le Monde
sources.framing_note: Article body not accessible

top_stories.headline: Hungary's President Sulyok Steps Down After Parliament Forces His Removal
bullets: President Tamás Sulyok signed a constitutional amendment ending his presidency at midnight Sunday after parliament passed the measure with an overwhelming majority
Prime Minister Péter Magyar's Tisza party pushed through the 17th amendment citing 'serious loss of confidence' in the president
Sulyok accused Magyar's government of violating the rule of law, calling it a 'breaking point in Hungarian constitutional democracy'
The amendment also removes the Constitutional Court head and bars MPs with three terms from standing again, affecting over half of current deputies

sources.name: BBC Europe
sources.framing_note: Balanced coverage noting both the government's justification and Sulyok's accusations of rule-of-law violations

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Same coverage as BBC Europe

top_stories.headline: Spain and Argentina Set for World Cup Final Showdown in New York
bullets: Spain and Argentina meet in the World Cup 2026 final at New York-New Jersey Stadium on Sunday at 3pm local time (19:00 GMT)
Spain has conceded just one goal in seven matches; Argentina has twice rallied from behind to reach the final as defending champions
Wildfire smoke from Canadian fires has created hazy conditions over the eastern US ahead of the match
England defeated France 6-4 in the third-place match on Saturday, with Bukayo Saka scoring a hat trick

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Focused on tactical contrast between Spanish control and Argentine resilience, coaching connection

sources.name: Al Jazeera
sources.framing_note: Emphasized the Messi vs Yamal storyline and 'clash of philosophies'

sources.name: The Economist
sources.framing_note: Framed as teamwork versus individual genius

sources.name: RFI
sources.framing_note: Detailed coverage of England-France third-place match

sources.name: Le Monde
sources.framing_note: Noted wildfire haze conditions

additional_stories.headline: São Tomé and Príncipe holds presidential election amid political tensions between incumbent and former ruling party
Seven American aid workers quarantining at controversial Kenya Ebola facility after US travel restrictions
Russians increasingly turning to cash as mobile internet shutdowns disrupt card payments, straining wartime economy
UK aid cuts reduce bilateral support to some African countries by up to 90%, Foreign Office figures show
Cuban dissident Luis Manuel Otero Alcántara arrives in US exile after serving five-year prison sentence
German politician Jens Spahn resigns as parliamentary group leader over use of US surrogate mother
New Zealand sending firefighters to help battle widespread US wildfires as over 850 fires burn across Canada
Trump threatens new tariffs on Canada over wildfire smoke, calling it 'Willful Negligence'
China's role as 'swing importer' helping cushion oil price spikes despite US-Iran conflict disrupting Strait of Hormuz
Israel demolishes Palestinian home in Masafer Yatta days after court order reportedly protected it
